data.text: Using the `ddx` skill because this is an execution bead and the tracker/merge rules are load-bearing. I’m checking the bead, the referenced backend files, and the local tests first to decide whether this is implementable in one pass or needs a no-changes outcome.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "ddx bead show ddx-9c5bca8f && printf '\\n---\\n' && rg -n \"axon_backend|backend_axon|TestAxonBackend|axon\" cli/internal/bead -g '"'!**/vendor/**'"'"

data.output:
ID:       ddx-9c5bca8f
Title:    bead/axon: implement backend operations with generated GraphQL client
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:feature, blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d, blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2, triage:needs-investigation
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Deps:     ddx-8bf23be0
Desc:     PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.
Accept:   1. TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet, TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists, TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim, TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree, TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection, and TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip pass against the real Axon-backed implementation.
2. backend_axon.go no longer uses local JSONL snapshots as the primary storage path.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
Created:  2026-05-05 16:19:59
Updated:  2026-05-05 18:45:14
Claimed:  2026-05-05T18:45:14Z
Machine:  eitri

Completing it would require coordinated changes across cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go, cli/internal/bead/axon/*, store/backend plumbing, and the axon-backed test matrix, while the bead is already at the depth cap. The requested real Axon GraphQL path is also still blocked on the upstream dependencies noted in the bead metadata (axon-05c1019d and axon-82b6f7b2), so I am not making speculative partial changes. created_at:2026-05-05T16:47:37.852529797Z kind:no_changes_needs_investigation source:ddx agent execute-loop summary:no_changes_needs_investigation] map[actor:erik body:no_changes

data.text: I’ve confirmed the bead is already marked as previously no-changed, and the task still spans the backend, generated client, and test matrix. I’m pulling the exact source lines now so the rationale can be grounded in file/line evidence instead of a generic blocker note.

data.output:
1	package bead
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"bytes"
     5		"encoding/json"
     6		"fmt"
     7		"os"
     8		"path/filepath"
     9		"strings"
    10		"sync"
    11		"testing"
    12		"time"
    13	
    14		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    16	)
    17	
    18	// newAxonStore returns a *Store wired to a fresh AxonBackend rooted at a
    19	// temp dir. Mirrors newTestStore so the existing high-level Backend method
    20	// surface (Create/Get/Update/Claim/Close/...) gets exercised against the
    21	// axon-shaped storage layout instead of the JSONL default.
    22	func newAxonStore(t *testing.T) *Store {
    23		t.Helper()
    24		dir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".ddx")
    25		s := NewStore(dir)
    26		s.backend = NewAxonBackend(dir, s.LockWait)
    27		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
    28		return s
    29	}
    30	
    31	func TestAxonBackend_InitCreatesCollections(t *testing.T) {
    32		t.Parallel()
    33		s := newAxonStore(t)
    34		ax, ok := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
    35		require.True(t, ok)
    36	
    37		for _, path := range []string{ax.BeadsFile, ax.EventsFile} {
    38			_, err := os.Stat(path)
    39			assert.NoError(t, err, "axon collection file %s should exist after Init", path)
    40		}
    41	}
    42	
    43	func TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet(t *testing.T) {
    44		t.Parallel()
    45		s := newAxonStore(t)
    46	
    47		b := &Bead{Title: "first", Description: "round-trip"}
    48		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
    49		require.NotEmpty(t, b.ID)
    50	
    51		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
    52		require.NoError(t, err)
    53		assert.Equal(t, "first", got.Title)
    54		assert.Equal(t, "round-trip", got.Description)
    55		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
    56	}
    57	
    58	func TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists(t *testing.T) {
    59		t.Parallel()
    60		s := newAxonStore(t)
    61	
    62		b := &Bead{Title: "to-update"}
    63		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
    64	
    65		require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(bb *Bead) {
    66			bb.Notes = "added by update"
    67			bb.Priority = 3
    68		}))
    69	
    70		// Re-read through a fresh Store wired to the same on-disk dir to prove
    71		// the change survives the in-memory closure.
    72		s2 := NewStore(s.Dir)
    73		s2.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
    74		got, err := s2.Get(b.ID)
    75		require.NoError(t, err)
    76		assert.Equal(t, "added by update", got.Notes)
    77		assert.Equal(t, 3, got.Priority)
    78	}
    79	
    80	func TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim(t *testing.T) {
    81		t.Parallel()
    82		s := newAxonStore(t)
    83	
    84		b := &Bead{Title: "claimable"}
    85		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
    86	
    87		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "alice"))
    88		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
    89		require.NoError(t, err)
    90		assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
    91		assert.Equal(t, "alice", got.Owner)
    92		require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
    93		assert.NotEmpty(t, got.Extra["claimed-at"])
    94	
    95		// A second claim against an active claim must fail.
    96		err = s.Claim(b.ID, "bob")
    97		require.Error(t, err)
    98	
    99		require.NoError(t, s.Unclaim(b.ID))
   100		got, err = s.Get(b.ID)
   101		require.NoError(t, err)
   102		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
   103		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
   104	}

   106	func TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree(t *testing.T) {
   107		t.Parallel()
   108		s := newAxonStore(t)
   109	
   110		root := &Bead{Title: "root"}
   111		child := &Bead{Title: "child"}
   112		require.NoError(t, s.Create(root))
   113		require.NoError(t, s.Create(child))
   114	
   115		require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(child.ID, root.ID))
   116		got, err := s.Get(child.ID)
   117		require.NoError(t, err)
   118		require.Len(t, got.Dependencies, 1)
   119		assert.Equal(t, root.ID, got.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
   120	
   121		tree, err := s.DepTree(root.ID)
   122		require.NoError(t, err)
   123		assert.Contains(t, tree, root.ID)
   124	
   125		require.NoError(t, s.DepRemove(child.ID, root.ID))
   126		got, err = s.Get(child.ID)
   127		require.NoError(t, err)
   128		assert.Empty(t, got.Dependencies)
   129	}

   131	func TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection(t *testing.T) {
   132		t.Parallel()
   133		s := newAxonStore(t)
   134	
   135		b := &Bead{Title: "with-events"}
   136		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
   137	
   138		for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
   139			require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{
   140				Kind:    "test",
   141				Summary: fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i),
   142				Body:    fmt.Sprintf("body-%d", i),
   143				Actor:   "tester",
   144			}))
   145		}
   146	
   147		// Events round-trip through ReadAll / Events.
   148		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
   149		require.NoError(t, err)
   150		require.Len(t, events, 3)
   151		for i, e := range events {
   152			assert.Equal(t, fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i), e.Summary)
   153		}
   154	
   155		// And the on-disk event collection actually carries the split entries
   156		// (TD-030 D3): one entity per event, linked back via event_of.
   157		ax := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
   158		data, err := os.ReadFile(ax.EventsFile)
   159		require.NoError(t, err)
   160		lines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace(data), []byte("\n"))
   161		require.Len(t, lines, 3)
   162		for _, line := range lines {
   163			var env axonEventEnvelope
   164			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(line, &env))
   165			assert.Equal(t, AxonEventsCollection, env.Collection)
   166			assert.Equal(t, b.ID, env.EventOf)
   167		}
   168	
   169		// And the bead row in ddx_beads must NOT carry inline events — events
   170		// live in their own collection per TD-030 D3.
   171		beadData, err := os.ReadFile(ax.BeadsFile)
   172		require.NoError(t, err)
   173		beadLines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace(beadData), []byte("\n"))
   174		require.Len(t, beadLines, 1)
   175		var env axonEntityEnvelope
   176		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(beadLines[0], &env))
   177		if env.Data.Extra != nil {
   178			_, hasEvents := env.Data.Extra["events"]
   179			assert.False(t, hasEvents,
   180				"bead row in ddx_beads must not carry inline events array")
   181		}
   182	}

   184	func TestAxonBackend_ListReadyBlocked(t *testing.T) {
   185		t.Parallel()
   186		s := newAxonStore(t)
   187	
   188		a := &Bead{Title: "a"}
   189		b := &Bead{Title: "b"}
   190		c := &Bead{Title: "c"}
   191		require.NoError(t, s.Create(a))
   192		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
   193		require.NoError(t, s.Create(c))
   194		// b depends on a; c depends on a closed-bead-to-be.
   195		require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(b.ID, a.ID))
   196	
   197		all, err := s.List("", "", nil)
   198		require.NoError(t, err)
   199		assert.Len(t, all, 3)
   200	
   201		ready, err := s.Ready()
   202		require.NoError(t, err)
   203		readyIDs := beadIDSet(ready)
   204		assert.Contains(t, readyIDs, a.ID, "a has no deps")
   205		assert.Contains(t, readyIDs, c.ID, "c has no deps")
   206		assert.NotContains(t, readyIDs, b.ID, "b depends on open a")
   207	
   208		blocked, err := s.Blocked()
   209		require.NoError(t, err)
   210		blockedIDs := beadIDSet(blocked)
   211		assert.Contains(t, blockedIDs, b.ID)
   212	}

   214	func TestAxonBackend_CloseAndArchivePolicy(t *testing.T) {
   215		t.Parallel()
   216		s := newAxonStore(t)
   217	
   218		b := &Bead{Title: "to-close"}
   219		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
   220		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "before-close", Summary: "x"}))
   221		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
   222	
   223		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
   224		require.NoError(t, err)
   225		assert.Equal(t, StatusClosed, got.Status, "closed beads stay in ddx_beads (TD-030 archive policy)")
   226	
   227		// Per TD-030 §"Archive policy under axon", closed beads remain in the
   228		// same ddx_beads collection; the events stay accessible via Events()
   229		// (the Store externalises them to the attachment sidecar on close).
   230		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
   231		require.NoError(t, err)
   232		assert.Len(t, events, 1)
   233	}

   235	// TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip verifies the bd/br interchange
   236	// contract: a bead exported through the axon-backed store and re-imported
   237	// re-creates the same bead corpus. This is the AC §"JSONL export/import for
   238	// bd/br round-trip" knot.
   239	func TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
   240		t.Parallel()
   241		src := newAxonStore(t)
   242	
   243		// Seed a small corpus with deps + events.
   244		a := &Bead{Title: "alpha"}
   245		bb := &Bead{Title: "beta"}
   246		require.NoError(t, src.Create(a))
   247		require.NoError(t, src.Create(bb))
   248		require.NoError(t, src.DepAdd(bb.ID, a.ID))
   249		require.NoError(t, src.AppendEvent(a.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "k", Summary: "s"}))
   250	
   251		var buf bytes.Buffer
   252		require.NoError(t, src.ExportTo(&buf))
   253		require.NotZero(t, buf.Len())
   254	
   255		// Write the export to a temp file and import into a fresh axon-backed
   256		// store. The expected count should equal the source corpus.
   257		jsonlPath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "exported.jsonl")
   258		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(jsonlPath, buf.Bytes(), 0o644))
   259	
   260		dst := newAxonStore(t)
   261		count, err := dst.Import("jsonl", jsonlPath)
   262		require.NoError(t, err)
   263		assert.Equal(t, 2, count)
   264	
   265		gotA, err := dst.Get(a.ID)
   266		require.NoError(t, err)
   267		assert.Equal(t, "alpha", gotA.Title)
   268	
   269		gotB, err := dst.Get(bb.ID)
   270		require.NoError(t, err)
   271		assert.Equal(t, "beta", gotB.Title)
   272		require.Len(t, gotB.Dependencies, 1)
   273		assert.Equal(t, a.ID, gotB.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
   274	}

   276	// TestAxonBackend_AtomicWriteSnapshotIntact proves WriteAll uses temp+rename
   277	// (writeAtomicFile) and does not leak partial state between writes.
   278	func TestAxonBackend_AtomicWriteSnapshotIntact(t *testing.T) {
   279		t.Parallel()
   280		s := newAxonStore(t)
   281	
   282		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
   283			require.NoError(t, s.Create(&Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("b-%d", i)}))
   284		}
   285	
   286		ax := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
   287		// No temp files should be visible after a quiescent WriteAll cycle.
   288		entries, err := os.ReadDir(ax.Dir)
   289		require.NoError(t, err)
   290		for _, e := range entries {
   291			assert.False(t, strings.HasPrefix(e.Name(), AxonBeadsCollection+".jsonl.tmp-"),
   292				"unexpected temp file %s", e.Name())
   293			assert.False(t, strings.HasPrefix(e.Name(), AxonEventsCollection+".jsonl.tmp-"),
   294				"unexpected temp file %s", e.Name())
   295		}
   296	
   297		all, err := s.List("", "", nil)
   298		require.NoError(t, err)
   299		assert.Len(t, all, 5)
   300	}
   301	
   302	// TestAxonBackend_ConcurrentClaimsSerialised reproduces the chaos contract
   303	// that two claimers must not both succeed: WithLock + writeAtomicFile must
   304	// keep the read-modify-write race correct under the axon backend.
   305	func TestAxonBackend_ConcurrentClaimsSerialised(t *testing.T) {
   306		t.Parallel()
   307		s := newAxonStore(t)
   308	
   309		b := &Bead{Title: "race"}
   310		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
   311	
   312		const goroutines = 8
   313		var (
   314			wg       sync.WaitGroup
   315			successN int
   316			mu       sync.Mutex
   317		)
   318		for i := 0; i < goroutines; i++ {
   319			wg.Add(1)
   320			go func(i int) {
   321				defer wg.Done()
   322				err := s.Claim(b.ID, fmt.Sprintf("worker-%d", i))
   323				if err == nil {
   324					mu.Lock()
   325					successN++
   326					mu.Unlock()
   327				}
   328			}(i)
   329		}
   330		wg.Wait()
   331		assert.Equal(t, 1, successN, "exactly one claim must win the race")
   332	
   333		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
   334		require.NoError(t, err)
   335		assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
   336	}
   337	
   338	// TestAxonBackend_SelectableViaConfigKnobWithFlag proves AC §3: the backend
   339	// is selected by beads.backend=axon (via DDX_BEAD_BACKEND) only when the
   340	// experimental flag is set. Without the flag, beads.backend=axon must fall
   341	// through to the JSONL default and not silently corrupt the workspace.
   342	func TestAxonBackend_SelectableViaConfigKnobWithFlag(t *testing.T) {
   343		t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND", BackendAxon)
   344		t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, "1")
   345

   348		_, ok := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
   349		assert.True(t, ok, "DDX_BEAD_BACKEND=axon plus the experimental flag must select AxonBackend")
   350	}
   351	
   352	func TestAxonBackend_ConfigKnobIgnoredWithoutFlag(t *testing.T) {
   353		t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND", BackendAxon)
   354		t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, "")

   357		s := NewStore(dir)
   358		assert.Nil(t, s.backend, "without the feature flag, beads.backend=axon must fall through to the built-in JSONL path")

   361	func TestAxonExperimentalEnabledTruthyValues(t *testing.T) {
   362		for _, val := range []string{"1", "true", "TRUE", "Yes", "on"} {
   363			t.Run(val, func(t *testing.T) {
   364				t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, val)
   365				assert.True(t, AxonExperimentalEnabled(), "%q should enable the flag", val)
   366			})
   367		}
   368		for _, val := range []string{"", "0", "false", "no", "off", "garbage"} {
   369			t.Run("disabled-"+val, func(t *testing.T) {
   370				t.Setenv(AxonExperimentalEnv, val)
   371				assert.False(t, AxonExperimentalEnabled(), "%q should not enable the flag", val)
   372			})

   375	
   376	// TestAxonBackend_OrphanEventsDropped verifies that an event entity whose
   377	// event_of points at a non-existent bead is silently dropped on read. This
   378	// is the partial-write recovery story documented in WriteAll.
   379	func TestAxonBackend_OrphanEventsDropped(t *testing.T) {
   380		t.Parallel()
   381		s := newAxonStore(t)
   382	
   383		b := &Bead{Title: "live"}
   384		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
   385		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "k", Summary: "live-event"}))
   386	
   387		// Manually append an orphan event for a bead that does not exist.
   388		ax := s.backend.(*AxonBackend)
   389		orphan := axonEventEnvelope{
   390			Collection:    AxonEventsCollection,
   391			SchemaVersion: axonSchemaVersion,
   392			EventOf:       "ddx-deadbeef",
   393			Index:         0,
   394			Event:         BeadEvent{Kind: "orphan", Summary: "orphaned", CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC()},
   395		}
   396		row, err := json.Marshal(orphan)
   397		require.NoError(t, err)
   398		f, err := os.OpenFile(ax.EventsFile, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
   399		require.NoError(t, err)
   400		_, err = f.Write(append(row, '\n'))
   401		require.NoError(t, err)
   402		require.NoError(t, f.Close())
   403	
   404		// Live bead's events still readable; orphan does not surface.
   405		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
   406		require.NoError(t, err)
   407		require.Len(t, events, 1)
   408		assert.Equal(t, "live-event", events[0].Summary)
   409	
   410		// And there is no phantom bead with the orphan id.
   411		_, err = s.Get("ddx-deadbeef")
   412		assert.Error(t, err)
   413	}
   414	
   415	func beadIDSet(beads []Bead) map[string]bool {
   416		out := make(map[string]bool, len(beads))
   417		for _, b := range beads {
   418			out[b.ID] = true
   419		}
   420		return out
